title: [InstCombine] Fold shift of boolean zext to logic sequence
author: stomfaig
description:
Alive2 proofs:
- `eq` case: 
  https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/09hPk-
- `ne` case:
  https://alive2.llvm.org/ce/z/zrof4X

Resolves llvm/llvm-project#180492

tests.test_body:
define i1 @icmp_sgt_ashr_zext_select(i1 %a, i1 %b) {
  %s2 = zext i1 %a to i8
  %s1 = select i1 %b, i8 4, i8 2
  %ashr = ashr i8 %s1, %s2
  %cmp = icmp sgt i8 %ashr, 1
  ret i1 %cmp
}

define i1 @icmp_eq_add_zext_select_i64(i1 %a, i1 %b) {
  %s2 = zext i1 %a to i64
  %s1 = select i1 %b, i64 13, i64 19
  %add = add i64 %s1, %s2
  %cmp = icmp eq i64 %add, 13
  ret i1 %cmp
}

define i1 @icmp_uge_or_zext_select(i1 %a, i1 %b) {
  %s2 = zext i1 %a to i8
  %s1 = select i1 %b, i8 10, i8 3
  %or = or i8 %s1, %s2
  %cmp = icmp uge i8 %or, 3
  ret i1 %cmp
}

define i1 @icmp_sge_shl_zext_sext(i1 %a, i1 %b) {
  %s2 = zext i1 %a to i8
  %s1 = sext i1 %b to i8
  %shl = shl i8 %s1, %s2
  %cmp = icmp sge i8 %shl, -1
  ret i1 %cmp
}

define i1 @icmp_slt_xor_zext_select(i1 %a, i1 %b) {
  %s2 = zext i1 %a to i8
  %s1 = select i1 %b, i8 10, i8 3
  %xor = xor i8 %s1, %s2
  %cmp = icmp slt i8 %xor, 5
  ret i1 %cmp
}

define <4 x i1> @icmp_ugt_mul_select_select_vec(<4 x i1> %a, <4 x i1> %b) {
  %s2 = select <4 x i1> %a, <4 x i8> splat (i8 2), <4 x i8> splat (i8 1)
  %s1 = select <4 x i1> %b, <4 x i8> splat (i8 2), <4 x i8> splat (i8 1)
  %mul = mul <4 x i8> %s1, %s2
  %cmp = icmp ugt <4 x i8> %mul, splat (i8 1)
  ret <4 x i1> %cmp
}

define i1 @icmp_ugt_mul_select_select(i1 %a, i1 %b) {
  %s2 = select i1 %a, i8 2, i8 1
  %s1 = select i1 %b, i8 2, i8 1
  %mul = mul i8 %s1, %s2
  %cmp = icmp ugt i8 %mul, 1
  ret i1 %cmp
}

define i1 @icmp_sle_lshr_zext_select(i1 %a, i1 %b) {
  %s2 = zext i1 %a to i8
  %s1 = select i1 %b, i8 4, i8 2
  %lshr = lshr i8 %s1, %s2
  %cmp = icmp sle i8 %lshr, 5
  ret i1 %cmp
}

define i1 @icmp_ne_add_sext_select(i1 %a, i1 %b) {
  %s2 = sext i1 %a to i8
  %s1 = select i1 %b, i8 5, i8 10
  %add = add i8 %s1, %s2
  %cmp = icmp ne i8 %add, 1
  ret i1 %cmp
}

define i1 @icmp_eq_add_zext_select(i1 %a, i1 %b) {
  %s2 = zext i1 %a to i8
  %s1 = select i1 %b, i8 13, i8 19
  %add = add i8 %s1, %s2
  %cmp = icmp eq i8 %add, 20
  ret i1 %cmp
}

define i1 @icmp_ult_sub_sext_select(i1 %a, i1 %b) {
  %s2 = sext i1 %a to i8
  %s1 = select i1 %b, i8 5, i8 10
  %sub = sub i8 %s1, %s2
  %cmp = icmp ult i8 %sub, 3
  ret i1 %cmp
}

comments.author: stomfaig

comments.author: github-actions[bot]
comments.body:
<!--LLVM CODE FORMAT COMMENT: {undef deprecator}-->

:white_check_mark: With the latest revision this PR passed the undef deprecator.

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: It looks like we even already have such a fold for icmp of add of sext/zext: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/blob/f81889da29eec1a70283986fc0a654b9d628157a/llvm/lib/Transforms/InstCombine/InstCombineCompares.cpp#L3137-L3161 Generalizing the foldICmpBinOpWithConstantViaTruthTable() fold would both subsume that and cover this case. And cover mix of ext + select.

comments.author: stomfaig
comments.body:
@nikic,

The rewrite you suggest does come with a lot of new test cases. In particular, the total test case space can be indexed with a cartesian product like

(select, zext, sext) x (select, zext, sext) x (supported binops) x (all icmp predicates)

Should I implement a test for all such cases (I can generate them using some script, that is not an issue, but there will be a lot)?

comments.author: nikic
comments.body: No, please do not test all cases. There should be at least one test per supported binop, but there is not need to test all combinations of select/zext/sext/predicates. You can just add a few representative tests spread across different binops.
